justifications.completeness: The extraction misses one of the two pedestrians and several vehicles present in the ground truth and also misreports at least one vehicle motion status, so coverage is poor.
justifications.semantic_richness: The extraction provides a variety of semantic attributes (actions, age, vehicle types, door/trunk states, communicative/transporting) that add detail beyond the ground truth, but it omits many objects and contains at least one incorrect motion label.
justifications.spatial_relations: Useful spatial relations are supplied (near wall, blocking road, behind truck) which add contextual information, though not all object relations are reported and some may be imprecise.
justifications.overall: The extraction delivers meaningful semantic detail and some spatial context but significant object omissions and incorrect motion information limit its overall reliability.

justifications.completeness: The extraction captures only one of four annotated pedestrians and two of five annotated vehicles, missing the majority of actors in the ground truth.
justifications.semantic_richness: For the objects it detects the extraction provides several relevant attributes (actions, age, door/trunk states), but it omits many actors and misrepresents or omits some motion statuses.
justifications.spatial_relations: The extraction supplies plausible spatial relations (e.g., blocking, behind) but these cannot be fully validated against the provided ground truth and some vehicle relations conflict with motion annotations.
justifications.overall: Partial coverage with reasonably detailed descriptions for detected entities but substantial misses in actor detection and some incorrect or unverifiable relation/motion information reduce overall quality.

justifications.completeness: Extraction reports one pedestrian and two vehicles while ground truth lists four pedestrians and five vehicles, so many agents are missing.
justifications.semantic_richness: Extraction captures a reasonable set of attributes per detected agent (age, atomic action, vehicle type, motion status, trunk/doors, communicative) but omits attributes for most ground truth objects and some motion states are inconsistent with the ground truth.
justifications.spatial_relations: Some spatial relations are present and plausible (near the store, behind the truck, blocking part of the road) but relations are sparse and not provided for most agents.
justifications.overall: The extraction provides useful semantic detail for a few objects but misses the majority of agents and many contextual relations, yielding a moderate-low overall quality.

justifications.completeness: Misses both pedestrians and three of five vehicles, so extraction is largely incomplete.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides some vehicle attributes like type and door status but misclassifies one vehicle and omits pedestrian attributes and several vehicles.
justifications.spatial_relations: Gives basic relations for the two reported vehicles but omits spatial context for pedestrians and other vehicles and contains vague phrasing.
justifications.overall: Significant omissions of pedestrians and multiple vehicles plus some misclassification yield a low overall score despite some correct attribute extraction.

justifications.completeness: Extraction captures only one pedestrian out of seven and three vehicles out of six, so it is largely incomplete.
justifications.semantic_richness: The output includes some attribute labels (age, motion, doors) but misses many actors and key context details and contains incorrect context labels, yielding limited richness.
justifications.spatial_relations: There are several spatial assertions provided, but they are sparse, partly speculative, and not well aligned with ground truth object identities or motion states.
justifications.overall: Overall the extraction is sparse and partially incorrect across entities, attributes, and relations, resulting in low quality for downstream use.

justifications.completeness: Detected 2 of 7 ground-truth pedestrians and 1 of 4 vehicles, missing the majority of objects.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides basic attributes like actions and age for detected agents but misses several important contextual labels (e.g., jaywalking, carrying, talking on phone) and many objects entirely.
justifications.spatial_relations: Includes a few spatial relations for some agents but omits relations for most pedestrians and vehicles, resulting in limited coverage and usefulness.
justifications.overall: Partial and sparse extraction with correct simple labels for a couple of detected objects but low recall and incomplete contextual and spatial details overall.

justifications.completeness: The extraction reports only 2 pedestrians and 2 vehicles versus 7 pedestrians and 3 vehicles in the ground truth, so most objects are missing.
justifications.semantic_richness: The VLM provides multiple attribute fields (atomic action, communicative, transporting, age, vehicle status) but many attributes are incorrect or absent for most ground-truth actors.
justifications.spatial_relations: Some spatial cues are present and partially match (eg right sidewalk, behind the van) but several actors lack relations and many context labels (walking on road, jaywalking) are not captured or are inconsistent.
justifications.overall: The extraction contains useful attribute structure but is incomplete and contains several incorrect action/context labels, yielding limited reliability for driving-scene understanding.

justifications.completeness: Many ground-truth entities are missing (3 of 5 persons and 3 of 5 vehicles) and several object-level attributes/tracks are omitted.
justifications.semantic_richness: The extraction includes a variety of semantic attributes (age, communicative, transporting, trunk/doors, types, track hints) offering good detail, though it misses at least one communicative label and some motion/track alignments.
justifications.spatial_relations: Spatial relations are provided for reported entities but many objects lack spatial annotations and several relations cannot be corroborated with the ground truth, lowering reliability.
justifications.overall: The output is semantically informative but suffers from low recall of objects and incomplete/partially unverifiable spatial coverage, giving a mediocre overall assessment.

justifications.completeness: The extraction contains only 2 pedestrians and 1 vehicle compared to 6 persons and 5 vehicles in the ground truth, missing the majority of objects and many per-object attributes.
justifications.semantic_richness: Some semantic labels like atomic action and age are present and correct for reported actors, but many context types (e.g., jaywalking, talking in group) and several object instances are omitted or not differentiated.
justifications.spatial_relations: The extraction provides informal spatial phrases for a few actors and a vehicle motion label, but relations are sparse, not aligned with ground truth motion/parked distinctions, and lack coverage.
justifications.overall: The extraction is partially correct for the few reported entities but is incomplete and lacks sufficient semantic and spatial coverage to match the ground truth.

justifications.completeness: The extraction misses at least one pedestrian and several vehicles and fails to report multiple vehicle motion states present in the ground truth, so coverage of actors and key attributes is poor.
justifications.semantic_richness: While many attribute fields are present (atomic_action, age, trunk/doors), the model omits critical semantics such as jaywalking and group communication and mislabels vehicle motion, reducing overall semantic fidelity.
justifications.spatial_relations: Some coarse spatial relations are provided but they are not consistently aligned with ground-truth relationships and lack coverage for several objects, making them of limited accuracy and usefulness.
justifications.overall: Overall the extraction provides a partial and sometimes inaccurate picture—useful basic attributes are present but missing actors, incorrect motion labels, and omitted contextual cues significantly limit reliability.

justifications.completeness: Detected 3 pedestrians and 1 vehicle while ground truth contains 7 persons and 4 vehicles, so many objects are missing.
justifications.semantic_richness: Extraction provides action, simple context, age, and vehicle door/trunk status but misses communicative labels (e.g., talking in group, phone) and several important contextual cues like jaywalking.
justifications.spatial_relations: Some pairwise relations are given but key spatial contexts from ground truth such as jaywalking and more detailed vehicle-object relations are not captured.
justifications.overall: Partial and somewhat detailed for detected agents but incomplete coverage and several missed or incorrect semantic and spatial attributes reduce reliability for driving perception.

justifications.completeness: The extraction reports only two pedestrians and two vehicles while the ground truth contains five persons and three vehicles, missing several actors and vehicle instances.
justifications.semantic_richness: The extraction includes many attribute fields but mislabels age (missing the senior), omits critical contexts like jaywalking, and introduces a bicycle not present in the ground truth.
justifications.spatial_relations: Spatial tags are present but are vague and fail to capture important relations from the ground truth such as jaywalking positions and correct vehicle motion contexts.
justifications.overall: The extraction provides some useful semantic attributes but misses multiple actors, misclassifies or invents entities, and fails to capture critical contextual relations, yielding limited utility for driving perception.

justifications.completeness: Misses multiple ground-truth pedestrians (2 of 5) and one moving vehicle, and lacks track identifiers, so coverage is limited.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides several attributes (age, atomic actions, vehicle motion and door/trunk states) but omits important contextual labels like 'jaywalking' and does not include track associations.
justifications.spatial_relations: Includes coarse spatial hints (left, center, on the road) that are partially correct but some objects are missing or their roadway relation is misrepresented.
justifications.overall: Moderate-low quality due to missed objects and missing critical behavioral/contextual labels despite reasonably detailed per-object attributes where present.

justifications.completeness: Extraction misses two of four pedestrians and one moving vehicle and mislabels one pedestrian action, so many ground-truth agents and behaviors are missing or incorrect.
justifications.semantic_richness: Extraction includes many attribute fields (atomic_action, age, communicative, transporting, doors/trunk) demonstrating decent richness but several semantic labels are incorrect or omitted such as jaywalking and a pedestrian labeled as standing instead of walking.
justifications.spatial_relations: Some reasonable spatial relations are provided (parked near store, car on road behind pedestrians) but others are unverifiable or possibly incorrect and several agents lack spatial relations.
justifications.overall: The extraction provides useful semantic detail yet misses multiple agents and contains notable attribute and action errors, resulting in moderate overall quality.

justifications.completeness: The extraction reports only 2 pedestrians vs 5 in the ground truth and 2 vehicles vs 3 ground-truth vehicles, so many objects are missing or unmatched and some object types are inconsistent.
justifications.semantic_richness: The output includes several attribute fields (age, communicative, trunk/doors) and spatial notes but fails to capture correct atomic actions and simple contexts for most pedestrians, reducing semantic fidelity.
justifications.spatial_relations: Spatial relations are provided for detected entities and some are plausible (e.g., blocking part of the road), but relations are incomplete and several pedestrian positions relative to the road are incorrectly or insufficiently described.
justifications.overall: The extraction is partially informative with useful attribute and spatial cues but suffers from many missing detections and incorrect motion/action labels, making it unreliable for robust perception.

justifications.completeness: Detected 2 of 4 pedestrians and 1 of 2 vehicles, so the extraction misses multiple object instances and has low recall.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides many per-object attributes (atomic actions, age, trunk/doors, communicative/transporting) but omits several entities and some correct simple contexts for missing pedestrians.
justifications.spatial_relations: Includes spatial relations for the reported objects and a plausible vehicle relation, but fails to capture relations for the missing pedestrians and misrepresents at least one pedestrian's road vs sidewalk context.
justifications.overall: Attribute labeling for detected objects is reasonably detailed but the low detection completeness and some context errors yield a moderate to low overall assessment.

justifications.completeness: Detected 4 of 9 relevant agents (3 of 8 pedestrians and the single vehicle), so many pedestrians were missed.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provided detailed attributes (atomic actions, simple context, age) for detected agents but missed the jaywalking classification and several agents entirely.
justifications.spatial_relations: Spatial relations given for detected entities are reasonable and consistent with the scene (center of road, near storefronts, far ahead) but many pedestrians lack relations due to missed detections.
justifications.overall: Good per-object semantic detail for what was found but low recall of pedestrians reduces overall usefulness.
